basic_details.job_title: DMER Maharashtra Tutors/Junior Residents Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 935 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Commissioner of Medical Education, Research & Ayush, Government of Maharashtra, Mumbai
basic_details.department: Medical Education, Research & Ayush, Government of Maharashtra
basic_details.short_information: DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026: Get the latest DMER Maharashtra recruitment notification 2026 for 935 Tutor and Junior Resident vacancies at Government Medical Colleges under the Commissioner of Medical Education, Research and Ayush, Mumbai. The online application process starts on 11th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 18th May 2026 (up to 11:59 PM). Find DMER Maharashtra vacancies on the official website www.dmer.maharashtra.gov.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026" notifications for fres

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for Tutor/Junior Resident posts are required to pay a non-refundable online processing fee. Fee payment is applicable only at the stage of filling online preferences (20–21 May 2026), not at the initial application stage. The fee must be paid online through the DMER portal only — fee submitted in any other manner will not be considered. Additional bank charges as per the concerned bank's rules will be payable over and above the processing fee. The Commissioner, Medical Education, Research and Ayush will not be responsible for technical difficulties during online fee payment.

age_limit.details: The upper age limit for Tutor/Junior Resident posts is up to 70 years as per NMC Gazette dated 30.05.2025. There is no minimum age specified beyond MBBS eligibility. Age relaxation for reserved categories is as per Government of Maharashtra rules.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions before applying. The minimum required qualification as per NMC regulations is an MBBS degree from a recognized medical college. All candidates must hold a valid Maharashtra Medical Council (MMC) registration. Age limit is up to 70 years as per NMC Gazette dated 30.05.2025. Educational qualifications must be from a Government or Government-recognized institute. Candidates must ensure they meet all prescribed eligibility criteria before paying the non-refundable processing fee. | Educational Qualification

salary_details.pay_scale: Tutor: Rs. 80,000/- Fix Pay | Junior Resident: Rs. 79,173/- Fix Pay

selection_process: Stage 1 Filling of Online Application 11-05-2026 to 18-05-2026
Stage 3 Filling of Online Preferences & Payment of Fee 20-05-2026 to 21-05-2026
Stage 4 Declaration of Allotment / Allotment Letter via Email 22-05-2026 by evening
Stage 5 Joining at Allotted Institute Within 7 days of allotment
Primary Merit Criterion Aggregate percentage of marks in First, Second & Third MBBS (Part I & II) taken together
1st Tie-Break Candidate with older admission batch preferred
2nd Tie-Break Candidate older by date of birth preferred
Specialization Preference Candidate with MD/MS/DM/MCH/DNB/CPS in concerned subject preferred over MBBS-only candidate; same tie-break rules apply among specialized candidates

The selection process for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026 is fully merit-based and online. There is no written examination. All eligible candidates are ranked strictly by merit based on MBBS marks (converted to percentage) in First
and Third MBBS (Part I & II) taken together. Based on the published merit list

candidates fill online preferences and pay fees. Allotment letters are then generated and sent via email/SMS. Candidates must join within 7 days of allotment. The posting order is valid for One Year only.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official DMER website https://www.dmer.maharashtra.gov.in. Hard copy applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online application portal will be active from 11-05-2026 to 18-05-2026 (up to 11:59 PM). Candidates must carefully review the detailed advertisement and confirm their eligibility before filling out the application and paying the processing fee. Information provided in the online application cannot be changed at any stage of the recruitment process — candidates will not be able to raise any complaint regarding this. To claim social or horizontal reservation benefits, candidates must indicate this in the online application itself; requests will not be entertained later.
Step 1: Fill online application form on the DMER portal (11–18 May 2026)
Step 2: Merit List will be declared on 19 May 2026 on the DMER portal
Step 3: Fill online preferences and pay processing fee (20–21 May 2026)
Step 4: Allotment for Tutor/Junior Resident posts declared by 22 May 2026 evening
Step 5: Join the allotted Government Medical College within 7 days of receiving allotment letter via email/SMS
Step 6: Submit originals of all uploaded documents at the time of joining for verification

Objections/representations regarding the advertisement must be submitted in writing with supporting documents within 8 days of publication to: The Commissioner, Medical Education, Research and Ayush, 4th Floor, Government Dental College and Hospital, St. George's Hospital Campus, P.D. Mello Road, Fort, Mumbai – 400 001.
important_instructions: Only online applications through the official DMER portal will be accepted; hard copy applications will be rejected. Candidates must hold a valid MBBS degree from a recognized medical college and a valid MMC registration. The processing fee is non-refundable under any circumstances; confirm eligibility before making payment. Information filled in the online application cannot be changed at any stage; candidates are solely responsible for errors. Candidates must claim all applicable social and horizontal reservations in the online application itself; claims not made in the application will not be considered later. All original documents must be produced at the time of joining; receipts/acknowledgements for pending certificates will not be accepted. Government Employees/PSU/Autonomous Body employees must produce NOC/Relieving Certificate at the time of joining. The posting order is for One Year only. Vacancy position is subject to change at any time. The Commissioner reserves the right to fill, postpone, cancel, increase or decrease positions, or modify terms and conditions at any stage. Candidates are advised to visit https://www.dmer.maharashtra.gov.in regularly for all updates. All detail information including specialty-wise vacancy, reservation criteria, and tie-breaker rules are available on the official DMER website.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: DMER Maharashtra has notified 935 vacancies including 650 Tutor posts and 285 Junior Resident posts in Government Medical Colleges under the Commissioner of Medical Education, Research & Ayush, Mumbai.

faq.question: Q2. What are the application dates for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application form is available from 11 May 2026 to 18 May 2026 (up to 11:59 PM) on the DMER recruitment portal.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for DMER Maharashtra Tutor and Junior Resident posts in 2026?
faq.answer: As per the notification, the fixed monthly pay is Rs. 80,000/- for Tutor and Rs. 79,173/- for Junior Resident, payable for the one‑year tenure.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS degree from a recognised medical college as per NMC regulations and must hold valid Maharashtra Medical Council (MMC) registration at the time of application.

faq.question: Q5. Is post‑graduate qualification (MD/MS/DM/MCH/DNB/CPS) mandatory for DMER Tutor or Junior Resident posts?
faq.answer: Post‑graduate qualification is not mandatory, but candidates with MD/MS/DM/MCH/DNB/CPS in the concerned subject are given preference over MBBS‑only candidates during merit and tie‑breaking.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 70 years in line with NMC norms, with relaxations for reserved categories as per Government of Maharashtra rules.

faq.question: Q7. How will the merit list be prepared for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: The merit list is prepared purely on MBBS marks, using the aggregate percentage of First, Second and Third MBBS (Part I & II), and all further allotment and preference filling are based on this merit ranking.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any written exam for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No written examination is conducted; selection is purely merit‑based through MBBS marks, followed by online preference filling and online allotment of Tutor/Junior Resident posts.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application / processing fee for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must pay a non‑refundable online processing fee of Rs. 1,000/- for Unreserved (Open) candidates and Rs. 900/- for Reserved category candidates during the preference‑filling stage on the DMER portal.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for DMER Maharashtra Tutor/Junior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible MBBS candidates must register and fill the online form on the DMER recruitment portal, upload MBBS and registration documents, wait for the merit list, and then log in again for online preference filling and fee payment, after which allotment letters are issued online.
